summary: First testing iteration. Backend: 10/10 pytest cases passed (health, profile photo GET/POST, invalid file type rejection, resume download, contact create/persist/list, validation errors). Frontend: Hero, About, Career Journey nav, Skills tabs (10 categories), Tech marquee, Projects (4 cards + dialog open/close via Escape) all verified working with real resume data (no dummy content). CRITICAL BUG FOUND: the floating glass navbar (data-testid='main-navbar') is horizontally mis-centered on ALL viewport sizes due to a Framer Motion + Tailwind conflict, making the theme toggle button and mobile hamburger menu button completely unreachable/unclickable (positioned off-screen beyond the viewport's right edge). This blocks theme switching and the entire mobile navigation menu.

ui_bugs.issue: CRITICAL: Header is centered via Tailwind classes `left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2`, but Framer Motion's `motion.header` sets an inline `style.transform` (from `animate={{ y: 0 }}`) that OVERRIDES/replaces the Tailwind transform (`transform: none` confirmed via getComputedStyle, while `left: 960px` on a 1920px viewport). This shifts the entire header ~576px (half its own 1152px width) to the right of true center. Verified: header rect right edge = 2112px on a 1920px viewport (192px beyond visible area) — theme-toggle-button bounding box x=2047 (fully off-screen, click throws 'Element is outside of the viewport'). On mobile (390x844), same bug pushes mobile-menu-toggle-button to x=508 (off-screen on a 390px-wide viewport) — the hamburger menu is completely unclickable, meaning mobile users cannot open the nav menu at all.

action_items: FIX ROOT CAUSE: In Navbar.jsx, the motion.header uses both a Tailwind transform utility class (`-translate-x-1/2`) for centering AND framer-motion's `animate`/`initial` props which set y-transform inline — these conflict because Framer Motion writes directly to element.style.transform, wiping out the Tailwind translate-x. Fix options: (a) wrap the centering translate in a parent non-motion div and only animate opacity/y on an inner motion.div, or (b) use framer-motion's `x` prop combined with `y` (e.g., initial={{x:'-50%', y:-100}} animate={{x:'-50%', y:0}}) so the transform framer writes includes both translateX(-50%) and translateY, or (c) remove `left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2` and instead center via flexbox/fixed inset-x-0 with mx-auto (no transform needed).

After fixing, re-verify: theme toggle button and mobile hamburger menu are clickable on desktop (1920x1080) and mobile (390x844) viewports, and scroll-progress bar / active-link highlighting still work correctly.

Retest theme switching (light/dark/system) end-to-end once navbar centering is fixed — this was blocked by the bug and could not be verified via click (route to test manually: dispatch click via page.evaluate as workaround was not attempted per no-force-fix policy).
Retest mobile responsive nav menu open/close once fixed.
critical_code_review_comments: email_utils.send_contact_notification is a synchronous function passed to BackgroundTasks.add_task — FastAPI runs sync background tasks in a threadpool so this is acceptable, but for consistency with the rest of the async codebase consider using aiosmtplib for a true async implementation.
server.py directly uses os.environ[...] (not .get with defaults) in email_utils.py — good practice for fail-fast, but ensure .env is always fully populated in all deploy environments.
profile photo upload never deletes/cleans up old uploaded files in UPLOADS_DIR — will accumulate orphaned images over time; consider a cleanup job or limiting to a single fixed filename overwrite.
No rate-limiting on POST /api/contact — could be abused for spam/email-flooding since it directly triggers an SMTP send.

success_rate.frontend: ~90% (all content/section features verified working; navbar centering bug is the sole critical blocker)

test_credentials: Not applicable - no auth system on this public portfolio site.
seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up TEST_-prefixed contact messages in MongoDB 'contacts' collection during backend testing (all removed after test run). Temporarily overwrote profile_photo setting with a test PNG during upload test — restored to original 'profile-current.jpg' after testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully solid (100% pass, real MongoDB persistence verified, email background task fires with no exceptions in logs). Frontend content/data is real and matches resume (no dummy data seen anywhere in Hero/About/Journey/Skills/Projects). The ONE blocking issue is the navbar horizontal-centering bug caused by Framer Motion overriding Tailwind's translate-x-1/2 - this affects EVERY viewport size and blocks theme-toggle + mobile menu entirely. Once main agent fixes Navbar.jsx centering, re-run: theme toggle (light/dark/system + localStorage persistence), mobile hamburger menu open/close + mobile nav links, and re-check scroll-progress bar & active-link highlighting still function. Other untested areas due to time: Experience Highlights counter animation, Recharts radar chart rendering, Certifications/Achievements cards, cursor glow effect, and full contrast check across all sections in dark mode (could not switch to dark theme due to the navbar bug) - dark mode is UNTESTED, prioritize this once nav is fixed.
rca_of_the_issue: Framer Motion's `animate` prop on `motion.header` writes an inline `transform` style directly to the DOM element (e.g. `transform: translateY(0px)`). This inline style takes precedence over the Tailwind utility class `-translate-x-1/2` which also sets the `transform` CSS property (via `transform: translateX(-50%)`). Since both attempt to control the same `transform` property, and Framer Motion's runtime JS write to `element.style.transform` wins over the stylesheet rule, the horizontal `-50%` translate is dropped entirely, leaving `left: 50%` un-countered. Result: header renders anchored from viewport-center-left-edge rather than truly centered, shifting entire navbar right by half its own width (576px for the 1152px-wide desktop header), pushing right-aligned controls (theme toggle, hamburger menu) off-screen.
